meta.description: Build clinical nomograms for individualized survival or risk prediction from Cox proportional hazards or logistic regression models. Inputs: survival or outcome data, predictor variables. Outputs: nomogram plot, C-index, calibration curve, prediction table.

scientific_integrity.detail: Scientific integrity check completed without reportable redline findings.

practice_boundaries.detail: Practice boundaries check completed without reportable redline findings.

methodological_ground.detail: Methodological ground check completed without reportable redline findings.

code_usability.detail: Code usability check completed without reportable redline findings.

functional_suitability.note: Build and plot modes, outputs, exclusions, and delivered artifacts align with verified behavior

reliability.note: Validation and SKILL_* reporting are strong; one progress log still appears before plot-path validation fails

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is solid; SKILL.md remains moderately long at just over 300 lines

agent_usability.note: Clear CLI examples, testing commands, and troubleshooting paths

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural for survival-analysis users; some domain terms still presume Cox or nomogram familiarity

security.note: File-path inputs are validated; no secrets, eval patterns, or destructive behavior were observed

maintainability.note: Script responsibilities are now cleanly separated and the common error-code names match the delivery standard

agent_specific.note: Strong trigger precision and composable build/plot contract; minor legacy-output friction remains

key_strengths: The skill follows a strong standardized R skill layout with separate main, validation, io, functions, and run_analysis layers.
Documentation, CLI help, smoke tests, and testthat coverage are aligned with the verified runtime behavior.
Common error-code names are now aligned with the delivery standard, improving consistency between the scripts and the troubleshooting guidance.
Output reproducibility is strong because the workflow captures a seed, session metadata, and deterministic file structure.

recommendations.title: Validate plot target before progress log
recommendations.problem: Plot mode emits a 'Rendering nomogram plot...' log line before validating that --plot_save ends with .pdf. This can make the failure point look slightly later than it really is.
recommendations.root_cause: The log call in run_analysis() occurs before save_nomogram_plot() performs the file-extension validation.
recommendations.fix: Move validate_pdf_output(plot_save, '--plot_save') to run before the plotting progress log, or validate the path in run_analysis() before logging the render step.

recommendations.title: Add one negative-path regression test
recommendations.problem: The current automated tests cover the successful CLI workflow well, but they do not lock in one explicit validation failure mode such as an invalid plot extension or missing column.
recommendations.root_cause: Testing is currently optimized for smoke coverage rather than guardrail regression coverage.
recommendations.fix: Add a small testthat case that expects SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER for a non-PDF --plot_save or SKILL_MISSING_COLUMNS for an invalid feature list.

recommendations.title: Retire legacy tests/output when safe
recommendations.problem: The skill now clearly standardizes on tests/expected_output, but the retained tests/output directory still creates a small amount of maintenance and discoverability friction.
recommendations.root_cause: Historical artifacts were intentionally preserved during standardization to avoid deleting prior outputs.
recommendations.fix: When those historical files are no longer needed, remove tests/output entirely or further de-emphasize it in all test-facing docs so tests/expected_output remains the only canonical output location.
